implementation_summary: Built app/scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs, a plain Node ESM codegen script (fs/promises + path + url only, never imports TypeScript) that globs app/src/themes/*/ for folders containing a manifest.ts, performs the two filesystem-derivable structural checks (manifest.ts present; folder name matches ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$), and emits app/src/themes/registry.generated.ts: one static import per theme (alphabetical folder-name order — the generator never reads `order`, so APP_THEMES/THEME_FONTS are correctly order-sorted at eval time by the emitted file's own hand-written helpers regardless of import-declaration order), the AppThemeName union, an _all array, and the order-sort/buildThemeFonts wiring. Every core function (findThemeFolders, validateFolderNames, renderRegistrySource, generate) is exported and parameterized on themesDir/outputPath so themes-validation.test.ts's AC-STRUCT-4 fixture tests can drive the real generator logic against temp folders without touching the real five themes; the CLI entry point is guarded to run only when the script is executed directly, not on import. Ran the generator to regenerate and commit registry.generated.ts, resolving MOCK-02-001 (the S2 hand-authored stub is now generator-emitted) with a fully preserved observable contract (parity.test.ts and compatBridge.test.ts pass unmodified against it). Wired `"prebuild": "node scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs && vitest run tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts"` into app/package.json (zod was already a devDependency from S1). Delegated to a dev subagent (base:dev) the full value-validation suite app/tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts (60 tests across schema/id, hardened contrast incl. non-hex fail-loud and colorScheme-never-trusted fixtures, folder drift against the pure registry.generated.ts export, order uniqueness, byte-identical font-URL parity, AC-STRUCT-2's lib/theme.ts re-export lock, AC-STRUCT-4's temp-fixture folder add/remove proof, and a generalized cross-directory zod-boundary scanner covering every client-bundle-reachable module plus the S1/S2 carried enum-catalog/treatment-sync and FontLoadSchema-structural-parity guarantees) and the forced AC-FONT-1 consumer edit to app/pages/_document.tsx (renders href from buildFontLinkHref(THEME_FONTS) instead of a hardcoded URL literal). The amended AC-VAL-3 (order-position sub-clause retired per spec.md's 2026-07-01 Amendments) was implemented as clause-1-only (folder-set drift against registry.generated.ts's pure APP_THEMES), with a code comment documenting the retirement and pointing to AC-FONT-1/AC-VAL-4 as the surviving metadata-drift coverage — no order-position comparison was written. Post-dev-spawn, the architect closed one real gap found during independent verification: AC-STRUCT-3's `--check` idempotence had only been confirmed manually via the CLI, so two automated regression tests were added (idempotent against the real committed file; throws against a deliberately stale fixture).

verification_summary.generator_evidence: node scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs regenerates registry.generated.ts (5 themes: calm, flower, lego, minecraft, playful — alphabetical); `--check` exits 0 against the committed file (idempotent), confirmed both via CLI and via two new automated tests (AC-STRUCT-3 describe block).
verification_summary.ac_dep2_evidence: Manually verified end-to-end: temporarily set calm/manifest.ts's id to an invalid value, ran `npm run prebuild` from a clean shell (not piped through another command that would mask the exit code) -> exit 1, 11 vitest failures cascading from the broken id. Restored the file, re-confirmed `--check` passes and the full suite is green (1779/1779). Separately ran a full, real `npm run build` (not just prebuild) against the VALID themes end-to-end -> exit 0, confirming prebuild's pre-script hook actually gates next build in the real npm lifecycle, producing a working static export at app/out with the correct built font URL in the rendered HTML.
verification_summary.ac_bound1_evidence: themes-validation.test.ts's generalized resolved-target scanner (own-directory-relative + @/-alias resolution) found zero runtime imports resolving to schema.ts across every manifest (discovered via findThemeFolders, not hardcoded), registry.generated.ts, buildChakraTheme.ts, fontUnion.ts, index.ts, all three treatments/* files, _document.tsx, useThemeStyles.ts, and ThemeIcon.tsx, with a 12-case self-test proving the resolver discriminates (not a false-pass). Independently confirmed empirically against the REAL client bundle: grepped app/.next/static/chunks/*.js from the full `npm run build` run above for `zod`/`ZodError`/`ThemeManifestSchema` -> zero matches.
verification_summary.lint: NO_LINT (unchanged since S0 — no ESLint config present in the repo)
verification_summary.regression_confirmed: app/tests/unit/themes/parity.test.ts, compatBridge.test.ts, and zodBoundary.test.ts (S1's early guard) all pass unmodified against the regenerated registry.generated.ts and the new _document.tsx/index.ts edits.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The story brief pre-specified an unusually complete design (generator emit format, --check semantics, every validation gate's fixture strategy, the exact zod v4 API path for reading enum options back from a nested schema shape) — closer to a detailed implementation ticket than a typical story brief. This made the work fast but meant most of the architect's own judgment calls were about scope boundaries (e.g. whether adding `ELEVATION_TO_VISUAL_STYLE` as an additive export to the S2 compat-bridge file was acceptable) rather than design tradeoffs. The one genuine gap requiring judgment — AC-STRUCT-3's `--check` idempotence not having an automated regression test after the dev spawn returned — was only caught by independently re-deriving and manually running the full verification checklist rather than trusting the dev's self-report; the dev's summary did not surface this gap on its own.
retrospective.surprised_by: Piping `npm run prebuild` output through `tail` and reading `$?` afterward captures `tail`'s exit code, not npm's — a first verification attempt at AC-DEP-2's negative path silently reported PREBUILD_EXIT:0 even though 11 tests had failed and vitest exited 1. Caught only by re-running without the pipe (redirecting to a file instead) before trusting the result.

absorbed_from.note: Dev's own retro was skipped (routine, no debug cycles) per the dev's final report — no substantive flag to absorb beyond the design-note judgment calls already folded into implementation_summary/verification_summary above (AC-VAL-3 drift-fixture approach, AC-STRUCT-2 line-pattern regex choice, FontLoadSchema access path via ZodArray.element).
